description:
coerce_to() currently panics on two valid transitions:

  (ArgRepr::RawInt, ArgRepr::RawF64)   => panic!("coerce_to: unsupported ...")
  (ArgRepr::RawF64, ArgRepr::RawInt)   => panic!("coerce_to: unsupported ...")

These combinations are not exercised by any fixture today, but will be reached
as the type system matures (e.g., a raw-int value passed to a continuation that
expects raw-float, or vice versa).

Fix: route through Tagged as an intermediate for both cross-raw transitions:

  (ArgRepr::RawInt, ArgRepr::RawF64) => {
      let tagged = box_int(b, val);
      unbox_float(b, tagged)
  }
  (ArgRepr::RawF64, ArgRepr::RawInt) => {
      let tagged = box_float_native(b, jmod, runtime, val);
      unbox_int(b, tagged)
  }

Proven: add a unit test that exercises both arms directly (construct the
values, call coerce_to, verify no panic and the result is a valid ir::Value).
All existing tests continue to pass.

## LANDING THE WORK
  Land this ticket before starting the next one:
  - Commit only the changes for this ticket, referencing fz-cg1.5
  - Close the ticket (`bw close fz-v9l.5`); It will tell you if work is newly unblocked
title: fz-cg1.5 fix coerce_to missing RawInt<->RawF64 arms
